statement.en: Symbolic Grounding Reveals Representational Bottlenecks in Abstract Visual Reasoning
statement.summary: Vision--language models (VLMs) often fail on abstract visual reasoning benchmarks such as Bongard problems, raising the question of whether the main bottleneck lies in reasoning or representation. We study this on Bongard-LOGO, a synthetic benchmark of abstract concept learning with ground-truth generative programs, by comparing end-to-end VLMs on raw images with large language models (LLMs) given symbolic inputs derived from those images. Using symbolic inputs as a diagnostic probe rather than a practical multimodal architecture, our \emph{Componential--Grammatical (C--G)} paradigm reformulates Bongard-LOGO as a symbolic reasoning task based on LOGO-style action programs or structured descriptions. LLMs achieve large and consistent gains, reaching mid--90s accuracy on Free-form problems,

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: The paper presents empirical findings on a controlled synthetic benchmark (Bongard-LOGO) with ground-truth generative programs. The core claim—that representational bottlenecks rather than reasoning capability explain VLM failures—is well-structured and supported by comparative experiments, but (1) generalization to real-world abstract reasoning remains unvalidated, (2) the diagnostic methodology (symbolic reformulation) is novel and not yet peer-validated, (3) the preprint date (2026-04-25) indicates this is pre-publication, and (4) the findings are contingent on the specific benchmark design and LOGO-style symbolic grammar used.

ramanujanFormula.interpretation: シンボルへの距離が深いほど、モデルの認識能力は指数関数的に抑圧される。
